title: Moschosma ocymoides
taxonomy: Moschosma ocymoides is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Moschosma, a small group of flowering plants. The species ocymoides suggests a close relationship with the genus Ocimum, which includes basil and related species. Within the Lamiaceae family, Moschosma ocymoides is a part of the subfamily Nepetoideae. Further classification at the infraspecific level may be necessary for a more precise identification.

intro: Moschosma ocymoides, also known as wild marjoram or Greek marjoram, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, Moschosma ocymoides has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Moschosma ocymoides is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and respiratory congestion.
